---
title: "Publications"
---

## Recent Publications

San Román, I. C., Fleming, I. A., Duffy, S. J., Holborn, M. K., Smith, N., Messmer, A., ... & Bradbury, I. R. (2025). Genetic monitoring suggests ongoing genetic change in wild salmon populations due to hybridization with aquaculture escapees. Conservation Genetics, 26(2), 347-360.

Ignatz, E. H., Xue, X., Hall, J. R., Islam, S. S., Rise, M. L., & Fleming, I. A. (2024). Defence‐relevant gene expression differences in hatchlings among wild Newfoundland and farmed European and North American Atlantic salmon and their hybrids. Molecular Ecology, 33(21), e17535.

San Román, I. C., Bradbury, I. R., Crowley, S. E., Duffy, S. J., Islam, S. S., & Fleming, I. A. (2023). Experimental comparison of changes in relative survival and fitness-related traits of wild, farm, and hybrid Atlantic salmon Salmo salar in nature. Aquaculture Environment Interactions, 15, 323-337.

Islam, S. S., Wringe, B. F., Conway, C. M., Bradbury, I. R., & Fleming, I. A. (2022). Fitness consequences of hybridization between wild Newfoundland and farmed European and North American Atlantic salmon. Aquaculture Environment Interactions, 14, 243-262.

Crowley, S. E., Bradbury, I. R., Messmer, A. M., Duffy, S. J., Parrish, C. C., Islam, S. S., & Fleming, I. A. (2022). Differences in energy acquisition and storage of farm, wild, and hybrid Atlantic salmon competing in the wild. Canadian Journal of Fisheries and Aquatic Sciences, 80(1), 43-56.

Find all of our publications at: <https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=8bBe9OYAAAAJ&hl=en>
